antibiotics.detail: gyrA S83L and parC S80I mutations detected — classic fluoroquinolone resistance signature.

literature.title: Target-site mutations driving fluoroquinolone resistance in Enterobacteriaceae
literature.source: Hooper DC, Jacoby GA · Cold Spring Harb Perspect Med
literature.quote: Mutations in gyrA and parC reduce fluoroquinolone binding affinity to DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV, the principal mechanism of clinical fluoroquinolone resistance in Enterobacteriaceae.

literature.title: Clinical impact of fluoroquinolone resistance in E. coli bacteremia
literature.quote: Fluoroquinolone-resistant isolates were associated with delayed appropriate therapy and worse clinical outcomes in bloodstream infections.

antibiotics.detail: blaTEM-1 beta-lactamase gene detected, consistent with beta-lactam resistance.

literature.title: TEM-1 β-lactamase and aminopenicillin treatment failure
literature.quote: TEM-1 β-lactamase hydrolyzes penicillins including amoxicillin and ampicillin; its presence reliably predicts aminopenicillin resistance in E. coli.

antibiotics.detail: No aminoglycoside-modifying enzyme genes found; genomic background associated with susceptibility.

literature.title: Aminoglycoside susceptibility in the absence of modifying enzymes
literature.source: Ramirez MS, Tolmasky ME · Drug Resist Updat
literature.quote: Absence of aminoglycoside-modifying enzyme genes is associated with retained gentamicin susceptibility, though phenotype should be confirmed given efflux- and permeability-mediated exceptions.

antibiotics.detail: Insufficient marker coverage near carbapenemase loci in this assembly — confidence too low to call.

literature.title: Genotype-negative carbapenem resistance via porin loss and AmpC hyperproduction
literature.source: Nordmann P, Poirel L · Clin Infect Dis
literature.quote: Carbapenem resistance can arise from porin loss combined with AmpC or ESBL production even when acquired carbapenemase genes are undetected, which limits the reliability of genotype-only calls.
